name: ntfy
description: Simple HTTP-based pub-sub notification service. Send push notifications to your phone or desktop via scripts.
summary: ntfy has seen rapid development in early 2026, with v2.21.0 (released 2026-03-30) adding verified email recipients via the `smtp-sender-verify` config flag to prevent abuse. v2.20.0 introduced S3-compatible object storage for attachments, and v2.18.0 added experimental PostgreSQL support as an alternative database backend — a major architectural milestone. Recent patch releases (v2.19.1, v2.19.2, v2.20.1) focused on PostgreSQL stability, UTF-8 handling, and S3 HTTP/2 compatibility. See full release notes at https://github.com/binwiederhier/ntfy/releases.

answers.What is the current license?: Apache 2.0 (server) and GPL (client), per project documentation — not explicitly stated in this changelog excerpt
answers.Is the project actively maintained?: Yes — multiple releases in March 2026 alone, with active bug fixes and major new features
answers.Does it support authentication and acces: Yes — access control is built-in; authorization queries are routed to read replicas, and email verification/access permissions are referenced throughout
answers.Is there a hosted cloud version availabl: Yes — ntfy.sh is the official hosted instance, and was upgraded to v2.18.0 as noted in the changelog

breaking_excerpts: v2.20.0: ntfy will take full control over the attachment directory or S3 bucket — files matching the message ID format with no database entries will be deleted. Do not reuse the attachment-cache-dir for other purposes.
v2.21.0: smtp-sender-verify flag introduced; ntfy.sh now requires email address verification before sending email notifications due to AWS SES suspension from unsolicited emails.
v2.18.0: Experimental PostgreSQL support added as alternative backend via database-url config option, representing a major internal restructuring (14,997 lines added, 10,202 removed).

alternatives.name: Pushover
alternatives.review: Paid SaaS, simple API, iOS/Android apps.

alternatives.name: Gotify
alternatives.review: Self-hosted only, real-time push via WebSocket.

alternatives.name: Apprise
alternatives.review: Apprise is a library that aggregates dozens of notification platforms but is a code library rather than a standalone pub-sub service, requiring more integration effort than ntfy.

alternatives.name: Pushbullet
alternatives.review: Pushbullet supports cross-device notifications and file sharing but is a proprietary cloud service with limited free tier and no self-hosting, contrasting with ntfy's open simplicity.

alternatives.name: Ntfy.sh (Unified Push / Matrix)
alternatives.review: UnifiedPush is an open standard for push notifications that integrates with self-hosted distributors including ntfy itself, but requires more ecosystem setup compared to ntfy's standalone simplicity.
